questions.text: What is the main criterion used by the World Bank in classifying different countries?
questions.explanation: The World Bank uses Per Capita Income (Average Income) to classify countries into high, middle, or low-income categories.

questions.text: The Human Development Index (HDI) is published by:
questions.explanation: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) publishes the HDI, which compares countries based on health, education, and per capita income.
BMI (Body Mass Index) is a tool used to calculate whether an adult is undernourished or overweight based on weight and height.

questions.text: Sustainable development refers to development that:
options: Meets the needs of the present without compromising the future
questions.explanation: Sustainable development ensures that resources are used carefully so that future generations also have access to them.

questions.text: Which state in India has a high Human Development Index despite having lower per capita income than others like Haryana?
questions.explanation: Kerala has a higher HDI due to its excellent performance in health and educational indicators like low Infant Mortality Rate and high Literacy Rate.
